title: Necessity: closure of the connectivity property under localizing operations not proved (fractional bound + desuspension)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Gap in Lemma `lem:forward` (necessity), current revised solution

After reducing to $H=G$, the proof asserts:
> "$\Phi^G$ preserves homotopy colimits and extensions and annihilates induced spectra $G_+\otimes_K Y$ from proper $K$, so the property '$\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G(-))\ge n/r_G$' (with $r_G=[G:\chi^{\mathcal O}(G)]$) is closed under the operations defining $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$; hence it suffices to check the generators."

The localizing subcategory $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ is by definition closed under **all homotopy colimits, retracts, and tensors with orbits** — which in a stable category forces closure under **cofibers**, hence (via the cofiber of $0\to X$ vs shifts) interacts with **desuspension**. The claimed closed property
$$\mathcal C := \{X : \mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G X)\ge n/r_G\}$$
has two problems:

**(a) Fractional / non-integer bound.** $n/r_G$ is generally not an integer; spectrum connectivity is integral. One must work with $\lceil n/r_G\rceil$ or, better, restate the invariant multiplicatively as $r_G\cdot\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^G X)\ge n$, and check this exact integer inequality propagates. The casual use of '$\ge n/r_G$' obscures whether the colimit-closure preserves the *integer* bound that ultimately yields $r_G\cdot\mathrm{gconn}(E)(G)\ge n$.

**(b) Closure direction.** Closure of a connectivity *lower bound* $\{X:\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^GX)\ge c\}$ under filtered colimits and extensions holds in the Postnikov $t$-structure, but it is NOT closed under desuspension, and the localizing subcategory is generated using cofiber sequences that can lower connectivity of intermediate terms. The argument must first establish $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq \mathrm{Sp}^G_{\ge 0}$ (connectivity bounded below), and then confine the closure argument to the connective range where $\mathrm{conn}(\mathrm{cofib}(f))\ge\min(\mathrm{conn},\dots)$ behaves well. None of this is written; the closure claim is simply asserted.

## Suggested fix
Define $\mathcal C_n:=\{X\in\mathrm{Sp}^G_{\ge0} : r_H\,\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^H X)\ge n\ \forall H\}$ (note: at all $H$, not just $G$, so the reduction-to-$H=G$ step is unnecessary). Show (i) all generators of $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}$ lie in $\mathcal C_n$ using $\Phi^H N^TS^1=S^{|T/H|}$, $|T|\le r_H|T/H|$, and the double-coset formula for $\Phi^K$ of induced cells; (ii) $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\mathrm{Sp}^G_{\ge0}$; (iii) $\mathcal C_n$ is closed under extensions and filtered colimits inside the connective part, using exactness of each $\Phi^H$ and the $t$-structure inequality. Then $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\mathcal C_n$ is exactly necessity.

## Difficulty
Moderate. The ingredients are standard ($t$-structure connectivity estimates, exactness of $\Phi^H$), but they must be assembled carefully, and the integer/fractional issue and the connective-confinement must be made explicit rather than waved through.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
Necessity now defines $\mathcal C=\{X:\mathrm{conn}(\Phi^GX)\ge c\}$, $c=n/r_G\in\mathbb Q$, and handles the fractional bound via $\mathrm{conn}\ge c\iff\mathrm{conn}\ge\lceil c\rceil$ (connectivity is integer/$\infty$-valued). $\Phi^G$ is exact and preserves colimits/retracts, so $\mathcal C$ is closed under (o1)-(o3); orbit tensors at proper $L$ land in $\infty$-connective via $\Phi^G(G_+\otimes_L-)\simeq0$. No desuspension is used (the subcategory is connective). This gap is closed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution of the closure-under-localizing-operations gap

I reviewed the current `lem:forward` (necessity) and verified it now correctly addresses both sub-concerns; I further hardened Step 2 to make the two delicate points fully explicit and self-contained.

**Key clarification of the framing.** The critic's worry (b) was premised on `tau^O_{>=n}` being a *localizing* (stable, bi-sided) subcategory, which would be closed under all cofibers/fibers and hence interact with desuspension. But the solution defines `tau^O_{>=n} = <generators>_{>=}` as a **connective** subcategory: operation (o1) forms colimits and **cofibers of maps between objects already in the subcategory**, and is explicitly **not** closed under desuspension. With this one-sided generation the desuspension issue is genuinely moot — one never needs the target property to survive `Sigma^{-1}` or general fibers.

**What I rewrote (Step 2 of lem:forward).** Made the integer/fractional and connective-confinement points airtight:

1. **Fractional bound (a).** Replaced the casual `conn >= c` by the equivalent **integer** membership: with `c = n/r_G >= 0` and `m := ceil(c)`, since `conn` is `Z u {+-inf}`-valued, `conn(Phi^G X) >= c  <=>  conn(Phi^G X) >= ceil(c) = m  <=>  Phi^G X in Sp_{>=m}` (the `m`-connective part of the Postnikov t-structure). From there the whole argument runs with the integer bound `m`.

2. **Closure direction / desuspension (b).** Spelled out the two estimates (o1) actually needs:
   - coproducts & filtered colimits preserve `Sp_{>=m}` (homotopy groups commute with them);
   - **cofiber of a map `f: A -> B` between two `m`-connective objects is `m`-connective**: the LES gives `pi_j C = 0` for `j<m` because `pi_j B = 0` (`j<m`) and `pi_{j-1} A = 0` (`j-1<m`). Taking `A=0` gives `Sigma^l B` for `l>=0`; extensions follow.
   I numerically/symbolically sanity-checked this LES estimate and the `ceil` equivalence. Then I state explicitly that (o1) only forms cofibers between objects in the subcategory, never fibers/desuspensions, so `Sp_{>=m}` need only be closed in this one-sided sense — which it is — and no intermediate cofiber can drop below `m`-connectivity. This is the precise resolution of the desuspension subtlety.

3. (o2) retracts and (o3) orbit tensors handled as before; for proper `L`, `Phi^G(G/L_+ ⊗ X) ≃ 0` (Step 1), so it lies in `C` unconditionally (∞-connective).

Steps 3-4 (generators in `C` via `Phi^G N^T S^1 = S^{|T/G|}` and `r_G·|T/G| >= |T| >= n`, then minimality of the generated subcategory) were already correct and are unchanged. The per-subgroup reduction (Step 0, via `lem:restr`) already gives the bound at **all** `H`, so the "all H" form the critic requested is in place.

Brace/environment balance checked; no LaTeX toolchain available to compile, but structure is sound.

I consider this gap closed.
